 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.9","title":"EPIC: Cockpit Runtime (ntm + multi-agent session ops)","description":"# Goal
Make multi-agent work *practically runnable* as a repeatable cockpit workflow.

This epic defines and operationalizes the “live” layer of the system:
- how we run Codex CLI / Claude Code / Gemini CLI side-by-side
- how those agents coordinate via Agent Mail threads
- how sessions are named, tracked, and wrapped up without chaos

---

# Why this exists (Brenner-aligned)
Brenner optimized for **evidence per week** and cheap, decisive moves.

A cockpit should:
- reduce context-switching overhead
- make parallel hypotheses cheap
- make it easy to compare representations across models
- keep the group honest (severe audience / third alternative)

---

# Key primitives
- **ntm** (Named Tmux Manager): live parallelism (spawn panes, broadcast prompts, capture output)
- **Agent Mail threads**: durable coordination + audit trail
- **Thread ID join-key**: thread_id ↔ ntm session name ↔ artifact file naming

---

# Non-goals
- Re-implement ntm inside Brenner Bot.
- Build vendor-API “agent orchestration”.

---

# Deliverables
- A documented cockpit runbook (operators can run a session without reading code).
- A session identity contract (thread id conventions; mapping to Beads IDs).
- Optional: a minimal `.ntm/` project template (prompt palette + helper commands) *if* it’s worth committing to repo.

---

# Definition of done
- A new operator can:
  - start a session (create thread id, choose excerpt, send kickoff)
  - run 2–3 agents in parallel in ntm
  - get structured deltas back into the thread
  - compile/publish the canonical artifact
  - perform a clean wrap-up (persist artifacts, update memory optionally)
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:03:08.665348793-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:03:08.665348793-05:00","labels":["agent-mail","cockpit","ntm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:03:08.695718585-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
@@ -378,7 +378,7 @@
 {"id":"brenner_bot-y6z","title":"Create BrennerQuote component with beautiful styling","description":"# Task

Create a stunning quote component specifically for Brenner quotes.

# Why Special Treatment

Brenner's quotes are the crown jewels of this project. They deserve more than gray italic text. Each quote should feel like wisdom worth savoring.

# Design

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ❝                                                      │
│    Exclusion is always a tremendously good              │
│    thing in science.                                    │
│                                                         │
│                           — Sydney Brenner, §42         │
│                                      [📋] [🔗]          │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

# Visual Elements

1. **Opening quote mark** - Large, decorative, in primary color (faded)
2. **Quote text** - Larger font, good line height, slightly italic
3. **Gradient border** - Left border with primary→accent gradient
4. **Background** - Subtle card background with glassmorphism
5. **Attribution** - Right-aligned, smaller, with § link
6. **Action buttons** - Copy, Share (appear on hover)

# Animation

- Fade + slide in on scroll reveal
- Subtle glow on hover
- Button reveal on hover

# Props

```tsx
interface BrennerQuoteProps {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  source?: string;        // e.g., '§42' or 'Interview, 1998'
  sourceUrl?: string;     // Link to transcript section
  variant?: 'default' | 'featured' | 'inline';
}
```

# Variants

- **default** - Standard quote block
- **featured** - Larger, more prominent (for landing page)
- **inline** - Compact, for use within paragraphs

# Accessibility

- Proper `<blockquote>` semantics
- `<cite>` for attribution
- Focus states on buttons
- Screen reader announces as quote

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Quote renders with all visual elements
- [ ] Animation on scroll reveal
- [ ] Copy button works
- [ ] Share button opens share dialog
- [ ] § link navigates correctly
- [ ] All three variants work
- [ ] Accessible markup","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:53.472975562-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.423890236-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.423890236-05:00","close_reason":"Folded into landing quote presentation (brenner_bot-9o2)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.812013867-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-2n1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.847436402-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-ypz","title":"CLI: add inbox fetch + thread summary helpers","description":"Extend brenner.ts with Agent Mail helpers: fetch inbox, read thread, summarize thread, ack messages, and basic polling.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T17:22:03.668949665-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T18:34:41.881677878-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T18:34:41.881677878-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.5.2 (CLI mail tooling)"}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-ysc","title":"Task: Implement swipe navigation between documents","description":"Add gesture-based navigation between documents on mobile.

## Purpose

Swipe navigation is a natural mobile pattern. Users expect to swipe left/right to go between items.

## Scope

- Swipe LEFT to go to NEXT document
- Swipe RIGHT to go to PREVIOUS document
- Only on document pages (/corpus/[doc])
- Threshold: 50% of screen width or 100px, whichever is less

## Technical Approach

### Option A: CSS Scroll Snap
- Wrap prev/current/next in scroll container
- Use scroll-snap-type for snapping
- Detect scroll end for navigation

### Option B: Touch Event Handling
- Listen for touchstart/touchmove/touchend
- Calculate swipe distance and velocity
- Trigger navigation if threshold met

### Option C: Library (react-swipeable)
- Use existing gesture library
- Less code, more reliable
- Small bundle impact (~3KB)

Recommended: Option C for reliability, unless bundle size is critical.

## Visual Feedback

- Show edge peek of prev/next during swipe
- Elastic bounce at first/last document
- Cancel swipe if released before threshold

## Accessibility

- Swipe is enhancement, not requirement
- Prev/Next buttons always visible
- Works without gesture support

## Files to Modify

- Create gesture wrapper component
- Wrap document page content
- Handle navigation events

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Swipe left/right navigates documents
- [ ] Visual feedback during swipe
- [ ] Threshold prevents accidental navigation
- [ ] Edge behavior at first/last doc
- [ ] Doesn't interfere with content scrolling
- [ ] Prev/Next buttons still work","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:13.330351376-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T07:21:30.339121607-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T07:21:30.339121607-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented swipe navigation on /corpus/[doc]","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-ysc","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0dk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:24.946600519-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-yz5","title":"Create Bayesian mapping visualization","description":"# Task

Build an interactive visualization of the implicit Bayesianism table from the README.

# The Table

| Brenner Move | Bayesian Operation |
|--------------|-------------------|
| Enumerate 3+ models | Maintain explicit prior distribution |
| Hunt paradoxes | Find high-probability contradictions |
| Third alternative | Reserve probability mass for misspecification |
| Design forbidden patterns | Maximize expected information gain |
| Seven-cycle log paper | Choose experiments with extreme likelihood ratios |
| ... | ... |

# Visualization Options

1. **Interactive table** - Hover for explanations, click for examples
2. **Animated flow** - Show the Brenner move flowing to Bayesian concept
3. **Card pairs** - Brenner move card links to Bayesian card

# The Objective Function

Display prominently:
```
                Expected Information Gain × Downstream Leverage
Score(E) = ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
              Time × Cost × Ambiguity × Infrastructure-Dependence
```

With explanation of what Brenner optimized (small denominator, large numerator).

# Design

- Clean table with subtle row hover
- Brenner moves in one color, Bayesian in another
- Expandable rows for full explanations
- Links to transcript quotes where applicable

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Table renders all 12 mappings
- [ ] Interactive hover/expand
- [ ] Objective function displayed
- [ ] Links to sources
- [ ] Responsive layout","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:05.719598117-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:28:08.487173181-05:00","labels":["method","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-yz5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:25.053470213-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-yz5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-twy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:28:08.431879875-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

2. **Animated flow** - Show the Brenner move flowing to Bayesian concept
3. **Card pairs** - Brenner move card links to Bayesian card

# The Objective Function

Display prominently:
```
                Expected Information Gain × Downstream Leverage
Score(E) = ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
              Time × Cost × Ambiguity × Infrastructure-Dependence
```

With explanation of what Brenner optimized (small denominator, large numerator).

# Design

- Clean table with subtle row hover
- Brenner moves in one color, Bayesian in another
- Expandable rows for full explanations
- Links to transcript quotes where applicable

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Table renders all 12 mappings
- [ ] Interactive hover/expand
- [ ] Objective function displayed
- [ ] Links to sources

 {"id":"brenner_bot-z9y","title":"Task: Document mobile UI issues","description":"Review screenshots and catalog all mobile UI issues.

## Purpose

Systematic documentation of problems before fixing. This prevents ad-hoc fixes that miss issues.

## Issue Categories to Look For

### Layout Issues
- Content overflow (horizontal scroll)
- Improper stacking
- Elements too close to edges
- Safe area violations (notch, home indicator)

### Spacing Issues
- Inconsistent padding/margins
- Elements too crowded
- Breathing room for touch targets

### Typography Issues
- Text too small to read
- Lines too long (> 65 characters)
- Truncation problems
- Poor hierarchy

### Interactive Element Issues
- Touch targets < 44px
- Elements too close together
- Hidden/inaccessible controls
- Unclear tap states

### Visual Issues
- Alignment problems
- Inconsistent styling
- Dark mode contrast issues
- Animation/transition problems

## Deliverable

Markdown document with:
- Screenshot reference
- Issue description
- Severity (P0 blocking, P1 high, P2 medium, P3 low)
- Suggested fix

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All screenshots reviewed
- [ ] Issues categorized by type
- [ ] Severity assigned to each
- [ ] Document committed to repo (e.g., docs/mobile-audit.md)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:59.387282661-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T02:13:29.405100227-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T02:13:29.405100227-05:00","close_reason":"Mobile audit document complete at apps/web/docs/mobile-audit-2025-12-30.md.  {"id":"brenner_bot-zos","title":"TanStack Table: Upgrade CrosswalkTable with Sorting","description":"# Goal
Upgrade the CrosswalkTable component to use TanStack Table for proper sorting, filtering, and accessibility.

## Background
CrosswalkTable shows a comparison matrix:
- Rows: 5 concepts (Problem Selection, Hypothesis Generation, etc.)
- Columns: 3 models (Opus, GPT, Gemini)
- Cells: Each model's interpretation of the concept

Current implementation is a simple map with hover states:
```typescript
const CROSSWALK_DATA: CrosswalkRow[] = [
  { concept: '...', opus: '...', gpt: '...', gemini: '...' },
  // ... 5 rows
];

{CROSSWALK_DATA.map((row, i) => (
  <tr onMouseEnter={() => setHoveredRow(i)}>
    <td>{row.concept}</td>
    <td>{row.opus}</td>
    <td>{row.gpt}</td>
    <td>{row.gemini}</td>
  </tr>
))}
```

## Why TanStack Table?
Even for a small table, Table provides:
1. **Sorting**: Click column headers to sort
2. **Column visibility**: Toggle which models to show
3. **Accessibility**: Proper ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation
4. **Future-proof**: Easy to add filtering, pagination later

## Implementation

### 1. Define columns
```typescript
const columns = useMemo(() => [
  columnHelper.accessor('concept', {
    header: 'Concept',
    cell: info => <span className="font-medium">{info.getValue()}</span>,
  }),
  columnHelper.accessor('opus', {
    header: () => <ModelBadge model="opus" />,
    cell: info => info.getValue(),
  }),
  columnHelper.accessor('gpt', {
    header: () => <ModelBadge model="gpt" />,
    cell: info => info.getValue(),
  }),
  columnHelper.accessor('gemini', {
    header: () => <ModelBadge model="gemini" />,
    cell: info => info.getValue(),
  }),
], []);
```

### 2. Create table instance
```typescript
const table = useReactTable({
  data: CROSSWALK_DATA,
  columns,
  getCoreRowModel: getCoreRowModel(),
  getSortedRowModel: getSortedRowModel(),
  state: { sorting },
  onSortingChange: setSorting,
});
```

### 3. Render with Table primitives
```typescript
<table>
  <thead>
    {table.getHeaderGroups().map(headerGroup => (
      <tr key={headerGroup.id}>
        {headerGroup.headers.map(header => (
          <th 
            key={header.id}
            onClick={header.column.getToggleSortingHandler()}
            className="cursor-pointer"
          >
            {flexRender(header.column.columnDef.header, header.getContext())}
            {{
              asc: ' ↑',
              desc: ' ↓',
            }[header.column.getIsSorted() as string] ?? null}
          </th>
        ))}
      </tr>
    ))}
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    {table.getRowModel().rows.map(row => (
      <tr key={row.id}>
        {row.getVisibleCells().map(cell => (
          <td key={cell.id}>
            {flexRender(cell.column.columnDef.cell, cell.getContext())}
          </td>
        ))}
      </tr>
    ))}
  </tbody>
</table>
```

## Enhancements Over Current

### Sorting
Click any column header to sort:
- Alphabetical by concept
- Alphabetical by model interpretation

### Column Visibility (Optional)
Add toggles to show/hide specific models.
Useful for focused comparison.

### Cell Click to Navigate
Make cells clickable to navigate to that model's full distillation.

### Keyboard Navigation
- Arrow keys to move between cells
- Enter to activate cell link
- Tab for focus management

## Considerations

### Styling
Keep the existing model-specific color coding.
Table primitives are unstyled - apply existing Tailwind classes.

### Mobile
On mobile, table may need horizontal scroll.
Consider: Card view on mobile, table on desktop.

### Row Hover
Maintain existing row hover highlight.
Table provides row.getIsSelected() etc. for states.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Table uses TanStack Table
- [ ] Column sorting works
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Existing styling preserved
- [ ] Model badges in headers
- [ ] Cell links to distillations work

## Files to Modify
